title: Daily Briefing — May 14
headline: Tech still driving, but flows hint at rotation while defensive sectors lag hard.
body: Right, so big picture: SPY ramps to $742.31 (+0.6%), well above its 20 MA ($720.03), QQQ pushing $714.71 (+1.1%). We're seeing relentless tech strength—AAPL, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, ARM all bullish and grinding higher above their 20s. VIX clocks in at 17.84, volatility still muted, and Fear & Greed sits at 64.4—greedy, but not extreme.

But look at the sector rotation: XLK (tech) is weakening in momentum, still dominant at RS=116.2, but starting to stall. Defensive sectors like XLU, XLP, XLV, XLF, and XLI are lagging and flagged HIGH risk or ELEVATED. Healthcare (XLV) and Financials (XLF) show 'improving' momentum, but Financials are still below the 20 MA ($51.80 vs $50.99)—the canary is not singing health. Watch for any mean reversion.

Contrarian note: Yes, tech is on fire and retail looks like it's piling in, but if it's in the press, it's in the price. Utilities, staples, and real estate all lag hard. No sentiment extreme triggers yet—Fear & Greed isn't at 75+, but you'd want to see breadth widen if this rally is to stick. If bonds freak out (HYG, LQD both soft), everything breaks, so stay nimble.
callout_body:
SPY: $742.31 (watch 20 MA at $720.03 as immediate support)
QQQ: $714.71 (mean reversion zone is $673.39)
NVDA: $225.83, active bull, well above 20 MA ($207.25)
XLF: $50.99—below 20 MA ($51.80), financials flashing caution
XLK: $176.85—tech still leading but watch for momentum fade
HYG: $79.91—just below 20 MA ($79.99); if this loses support, cut risk
action_items: Tighten stops on high-flying tech names—momentum weakening and sector leadership may rotate.
Monitor XLF (Financials) and HYG—any breakdown below today’s levels triggers defensive shift per macro rule.
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict—abundance mindset, next trade always comes.
